source_jobs.title: Sales Executive
source_jobs.description:
At SiteMinder we believe the individual contributions of our employees are what drive our success. That’s why we hire and encourage diverse teams that include and respect a variety of voices, identities, backgrounds, experiences and perspectives. Our diverse and inclusive culture enables our employees to bring their unique selves to work and be proud of doing so. It’s in our differences that we will keep revolutionising the way for our customers. We are better together!



What We Do…

We’re people who love technology but know that hoteliers just want things to be simple. So since 2006 we’ve been constantly innovating our world-leading hotel commerce platform to help accommodation owners find and book more guests online - quickly and simply.

 

We’ve helped everyone from boutique hotels to big chains, enabling travellers to book igloos, cabins, castles, holiday parks, campsites, pubs, resorts, Airbnbs, and everything in between.

 

And today, we’re the world’s leading open hotel commerce platform, supporting 50,000 hotels in 150+ countries - with over 130 million reservations processed by SiteMinder’s technology every year.



Ready to Shake Up the Hotel World?

Forget boring sales jobs. We're on a mission to help hotel owners ditch the admin headaches and get back to doing what they love – being great hosts. That’s where you come in.

As a Sales Executive at SiteMinder, you'll be a hero to small and medium-sized hoteliers. You'll connect with them, understand their challenges, and show them how our game-changing tech can transform their business, boost their revenue, and simplify their lives. You're not just selling software; you're selling a solution you can believe in.

Worried about being thrown in the deep end? Don't be.

We invest in you, big time. You'll kick things off with a month-long training and onboarding program that will make you an expert on our products and the industry. From day one, you'll have a dedicated sales coach, a buddy, and a team leader in your corner, all cheering you on and helping you smash your goals.

Your Mission!

 - Become a Deal Hunter: Uncover new opportunities and show our existing customers some love with killer upsells.

 - Smash Your Targets: Drive sales from start to finish and celebrate the wins with the team.

 - Wow & Win: Craft proposals that impress and negotiate contracts where everyone walks away happy.

 - Keep it Clean: Maintain your pipeline in our CRM (a tidy pipeline is a happy pipeline!).

 - Be the Voice: Channel client feedback straight to our product gurus to make our tech even better.

What we need from you:

 - Software-as-a-Service (SaaS) sales experience would be brilliant but a passion to sell is the key to this role.

 - Fluent in English (written and spoken)

 - A confident phone presence, fearlessness in leading meetings with prospects, and great listening skills 

 - Strong verbal skills to communicate professionally with customers, in writing and over the phone 

 - Self-motivation and resilience to show up each day and give your best 

Our Perks & Benefits…

 - Equity packages for you to be a part of the SiteMinder journey 

 - Hybrid working model (3 days per week at our London Hammersmith office)

 - Mental health and well-being initiatives

 - Generous parental (including secondary) leave policy

 - Paid birthday, study and volunteering leave every year

 - Sponsored social clubs, team events, and celebrations

 - Employee Resource Groups (ERG) to help you connect and get involved 

 - Investment in your personal growth offering training for your advancement

Does this job sound like you? If yes, we'd love for you to be part of our team! Please send a copy of your resume and our Talent Acquisition team will be in touch.



When you apply, please tell us the pronouns you use and any adjustments you may need during the interview process. We encourage people from underrepresented groups to apply.

About the Senior Talent Acquisition Consultant...

The Senior Talent Acquisition Consultant is the cultural representative of SiteMinder and our employer brand. This person is responsible for showing the active and passive candidate market what it’s like to be part of the SiteMinder team. This person will be engaging with prospective employees throughout AMERS and LATAM for various teams.



What you'll do

 - Own the end to end recruitment process across AMERS and LATAM, for GTM teams plus other departments when needed

 - Responsible for developing and executing on sourcing strategies that deliver the strongest results and make sure we’re bringing top talent into the business

 - Ability to understand what the company does and how each role fits into the business - qualifying quality candidates against the company, role and team culture

 - Partner with hiring managers - building trust, keeping them updated on the talent market, implementing their feedback, giving advice when needed, demonstrating an understanding of their hiring needs and keeping them informed when hiring

 - Work as part of a global TA team - while you will spend most of your day working independently, you also have a team to support you

 - Provide a world-class candidate experience - our company culture is demonstrated from the first contact

 - Keep records updated, providing input for important reporting metrics and always trying to improve the recruitment process

 - Manage up - knowing when to escalate potential issues, communicating with your manager and asking for support before it’s too late



What you'll have

 - Previous experience hiring for GTM teams, in an in-house recruitment role

 - Strong background in headhunting, sourcing and building talent pipelines

 - Able to influence and work with a wide range of stakeholders

 - High attention to detail when working with the ATS

 - A passion for delivering a stellar candidate experience

 - Enjoys working with people and candidates from around the world

 - Are data driven and can have data lead conversations

 - Excellent communications skills in English



Please send us your CV in English

About the Hospitality Solutions Consultant (Hotel Tech)



We are looking for a high-performing Hospitality Solutions Consultant to join our North America team in Irving, Texas. This is a client-facing, pre-sales role focused on supporting enterprise and mid-market hotel opportunities by bringing deep hospitality expertise into the sales process. You will partner closely with Account Executives to translate hotel operations, revenue strategy, and distribution needs and opportunities into clear, value-focused solutions powered by SiteMinder's platform.



What you'll do…



 - Partner with Account Executives on discovery calls to identify hotel operational pain points, revenue gaps, and distribution inefficiencies.

 - Act as a subject matter expert in hotel operations, revenue management, and hospitality technology throughout the sales cycle.

 - Support mid-to-late stage opportunities by clearly articulating business value, ROI, and measurable outcomes.

 - Deliver tailored product demonstrations aligned to each hotel's size, segment, and distribution strategy.

 - Translate platform capabilities into outcomes such as increased direct bookings, reduced OTA dependency, and improved operational efficiency.

 - Respond effectively to technical and operational questions from hotel stakeholders during the sales process.

 - Train and enable Sales teams on hospitality fundamentals including RevPAR, ADR, channel mix, and hotel tech ecosystems.

 - Contribute to sales playbooks and best practices across different hotel segments (privately owned, boutique, enterprise, multi-property groups).

 - Help elevate the overall consultative selling approach across the North America sales organization.



What you have…



 - Extensive experience in one or more of the following: hotel operations, revenue management, or hospitality technology (Solutions Consultant / Sales Engineer / Customer Success).

 - Strong understanding of hotel systems and ecosystem including PMS, channel managers, booking engines, CRS, and distribution platforms.

 - Proven ability to connect operational needs to measurable business outcomes and revenue impact.

 - Experience supporting or participating in complex B2B SaaS sales cycles in a pre-sales or consultative role.

 - Strong presentation and communication skills with the ability to engage both operational and executive-level stakeholders.

 - Ability to manage multiple opportunities in parallel with strong organization and prioritization skills.

 - Commercial mindset with the ability to contribute to deal strategy and support revenue outcomes.

 - Experience delivering product demonstrations or solution presentations in a client-facing environment.

 - Strong understanding of hospitality distribution and revenue management concepts (mandatory).

 - Familiarity with integration ecosystem

We’ve helped everyone from boutique hotels to big chains, enabling travellers to book igloos, cabins, castles, holiday parks, campsites, pubs, resorts, Airbnbs, and everything in between.

 

And today, we’re the world’s leading open hotel commerce platform, supporting 50,000 hotels in 150+ countries - with over 130 million reservations processed by SiteMinder’s technology every year.



About the Senior Site Reliability Engineer role…

We are seeking a Senior Site Reliability Engineer who brings technical-lead and architect-level expertise to our data platform infrastructure. This is an individual-contributor (IC) role based in Pune, working in-office with our Data Engineering and Data Science teams.

Day-to-day, you'll contribute to the architecture of the platform — AWS accounts, Kubernetes clusters, Terraform-managed services, and the observability stack that powers the data org. You'll help shape technical direction, partner directly with Data Engineers and Data Scientists as their dedicated infrastructure SME, and bring the depth needed to translate their requirements into scalable, secure, production-grade systems.

As a key member of the Infrastructure Platform team, you will be the on-ground Subject Matter Expert in:

• AWS Cloud infrastructure

• Kubernetes administration

• Terraform for Infrastructure-as-Code



What you’ll do…

 - Act as the embedded infrastructure SME for Data Engineering and Data Science — partner on design, unblock the data org's needs, and bring infrastructure judgement into their roadmap.

 - Architect, build, and maintain scalable and reliable production-grade infrastructure using Terraform.

 - Monitor, troubleshoot, and optimise the infrastructure platform for performance and cost.

 - Collaborate with data scientists and engineers to understand their data requirements and provide efficient solutions, adhering to security policies.

 - Maintain and audit security of the infrastructure (both network-level and system hardening).

 - Operate within and contribute to security standards and regulatory frameworks such as PCI DSS, GDPR, and ISO 27001, in partnership with the security team.

 - Evaluate and recommend new technologies and tools to improve the infrastructure platform.

 - Develop and maintain comprehensive documentation for all platform components.

 - Act as an escalation point and subject matter expert for BAU support issues related to the infrastructure platform.

 - Participate in 24×7 on-call rotation to support critical production infrastructure.
   
   

What you have…

 - Extensive experience in infrastructure platform engineering (SRE, DevOps, Platform), including experience in a Tech Lead, Architect, or equivalent technical-authority role. Track record of owning architecture decisions end-to-end — design, trade-off articulation, standards-setting, and cross-team influence with Engineering and Data teams.

 - Strong proficiency in at least one programming language (e.g. Python, Go).

 - Strong Linux administration skills and security hardening experience.

 - Demonstrated deep experience with Terraform at scale — modules, remote state strategy, environment patterns, CI/CD integration, and governance (Sentinel / OPA / policy-as-code

source_jobs.title: Senior Payroll Associate
source_jobs.description:
At SiteMinder we believe the individual contributions of our employees are what drive our success. That’s why we hire and encourage diverse teams that include and respect a variety of voices, identities, backgrounds, experiences and perspectives. Our diverse and inclusive culture enables our employees to bring their unique selves to work and be proud of doing so. It’s in our differences that we will keep revolutionising the way for our customers. We are better together!



What We Do…

We’re people who love technology but know that hoteliers just want things to be simple. So since 2006 we’ve been constantly innovating our world-leading hotel commerce platform to help accommodation owners find and book more guests online - quickly and simply.

 

We’ve helped everyone from boutique hotels to big chains, enabling travellers to book igloos, cabins, castles, holiday parks, campsites, pubs, resorts, Airbnbs, and everything in between.

 

And today, we’re the world’s leading open hotel commerce platform, supporting 50,000 hotels in 150+ countries - with over 130 million reservations processed by SiteMinder’s technology every year.





About the Senior Payroll Associate role...

As our team continues to grow, we’re hiring Senior Payroll Associates to join our Payroll team in the Philippines, a key hub in our evolving global payroll operations.

This role is for resilient, adaptable payroll professionals who stay steady in fast-paced, high-volume environments and are energised by change, complexity, and continuous improvement.

You’ll take full ownership of end-to-end payroll while helping shape a more centralised, scalable payroll function across multiple countries. It’s for someone comfortable working through ambiguity, solving issues as they arise, and continuously improving how things are done.

You’ll play a key part in ensuring accurate and timely payroll delivery, while also contributing to process stability and operational improvements across the business.



What you'll do:

 - Own end-to-end payroll processing with a focus on accuracy, compliance, and timeliness

 - Support multi-country payroll operations as part of a centralisation journey

 - Coordinate payroll inputs across HR, Finance, and key stakeholders

 - Manage payroll activities including benefits and compensation administration

 - Identify gaps and drive continuous process improvement and stabilisation

 - Adapt quickly to shifting priorities and evolving country requirements

 - Maintain strict confidentiality across all payroll and employee data



What you have:

 - 5+ years of end-to-end payroll experience, ideally in a senior or autonomous role

 - Strong Philippine payroll expertise, with multi-country exposure as a plus

 - Experience handling high-volume payroll (1,000+ employees preferred)

 - Solid understanding of benefits and compensation processes

 - Experience in fast-paced environments (BPO/shared services highly valued)

 - Familiarity with Sprout payroll system is a plus

 - Above all: resilience, adaptability, and confidence working through complexity



Our Perks & Benefits:

 - Equity so you can share in SiteMinder’s growth and success

 - Hybrid working model (office + remote flexibility)

 - Mental health and wellbeing support initiatives

 - Generous parental leave, including secondary carers

 - Paid birthday, study, and volunteering leave each year

 - Team events, social clubs, and global celebrations

 - Employee Resource Groups (ERGs) for connection and community

 - Ongoing investment in your growth, learning, and leadership development

Does this job sound like you? If yes, we'd love for you to be part of our team! Please send a copy of your resume and our Talent Acquisition team will be in touch.



When you apply, please tell us the pronouns you use and any adjustments you may need during the interview process. We encourage people from underrepresented groups to apply.

notes: High volume of sales and business development roles, with some tech roles present.
